entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The target crop [Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp.] for shoot regeneration and genetic transformation.

entities.name: SHOOT REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of producing multiple adventitious shoots from leaf explants.

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of introducing foreign genes into the pigeonpea genome.

entities.name: LEAF EXPLANTS
entities.description: Plant tissue derived from 4- to 5-day-old in vitro raised seedlings used for regeneration.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC PLANTS
entities.description: Plants produced through genetic transformation containing foreign DNA.

entities.name: SHOOT INDUCTION MEDIUM
entities.description: Medium consisting of MS medium, benzyladenine, and kinetin used to induce shoots.

entities.name: MURASHIGE AND SKOOG MEDIUM
entities.description: A basal nutrient medium used for plant tissue culture.

entities.name: BENZYLADENINE
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the shoot induction medium to promote shoot growth.

entities.name: KINETIN
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the shoot induction medium to promote shoot growth.

entities.name: GIBBERELLIC ACID
entities.description: A plant hormone used to promote the elongation of shoot buds.

entities.name: INDOLE-3-ACETIC ACID
entities.description: An auxin used in MS medium to promote rooting of elongated shoots.

entities.name: BIOLISTICS-MEDIATED GENE TRANSFER
entities.description: A method of genetic transformation using a gene gun to deliver DNA.

entities.name: NPTII
entities.description: A marker gene used for the selection of transformed pigeonpea plants.

entities.name: UIDA
entities.description: A reporter gene used to demonstrate gene integration and expression.

entities.name: GENE INTEGRATION
entities.description: The successful incorporation of foreign DNA into the plant genome.

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The process by which the integrated foreign gene is transcribed and translated.
